channel.title: NYT > Arts
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Rush Lost Its Beloved Drummer. The Band Is Ready to Get Back on the Road.
item.description: After losing Neil Peart to cancer six years ago, the trio is returning to touring with Anika Nilles, a powerhouse drummer who’s forged her own relationship with the group.
item.dc:creator: Hank Shteamer
item.media:credit: Nathan Cyprys for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left: Geddy Lee, Anika Nilles, Alex Lifeson and Loren Gold. The new touring lineup of the band Rush will hit the road in June.

item.title: Venice Biennale Jury Resigns Amid Tension Over Awards Ban
item.description: The jury of the world’s most important art exhibition had said it wouldn’t consider artists from countries whose leaders are accused of crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Stefano Mazzola/Getty Images
item.media:description: The Israel pavilion at the Venice Biennale in 2024.

item.title: ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Review: A Rhapsody in Cerulean
item.description: In this sequel, Andy (Anne Hathaway) and Miranda (Meryl Streep) encounter a series of crises that set the stage for a larger, existential catastrophe.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Top 10 Reasons We Love the Original ‘Devil Wears Prada’ Movie
item.description: The 2006 comedy is filled with moments big and small that have stood the test of time, thanks in no small part to Meryl Streep’s turn as Miranda Priestly.
item.dc:creator: Maya Phillips

item.title: A ‘Bachelorette’ Season Hangs in the Balance as Abuse Claims Are Aired
item.description: Television executives have not ruled out a revival of Taylor Frankie Paul’s season of the reality show, but a custody battle has kept her volatile former relationship in the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.category: The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Bethany Baker
item.media:description: Taylor Frankie Paul’s lawyers had sought to prevent Thursday’s court hearing from being streamed online by news media, but the court declined to grant the request.

item.title: August Wilson Like You’ve Never Heard Him Before: In Italian
item.description: In three U.S. cities, a new production of the playwright’s cabdriver drama “Jitney” will be imported from Italy.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg and Maurizio Fiorino
item.media:credit: Maurizio Fiorino for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rosanna Sparapano and Miguel Gobbo Diaz, actors in a new revival of “Jitney,” at the final dress rehearsal of the Sardegna Teatro production in Sardinia, Italy.

item.title: ‘Wowsabout’ Looks to Nature, and Jim Henson, in Hopes of Inspiring Awe
item.description: Produced by the Jim Henson Company in Sequoia National Park, the PBS Kids special leads a series of programs tied to America’s 250th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Graeber
item.media:credit: The Jim Henson Company
item.media:description: Ronald the pig and Roxy the hedgehog lead “Wowsabout,” a new children’s special about the awe-inspiring power of nature that producers hope will expand into a series.

item.title: ‘Animal Farm’ Review: George Orwell, Lost in the Mud
item.description: A confusing adaptation of Orwell’s political novel seems blissfully detached from its source’s clarity.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: Fortnite Issues Refunds for D4vd Gameplay Items
item.description: The singer, who has been charged with the murder of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, began his career making music inspired by the game.
item.dc:creator: Reggie Ugwu
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Ted Soqui - Pool/Getty Images
item.media:description: David Burke has pleaded not guilty to a first-degree murder charge in connection with the killing of Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

item.title: Michelangelo and Rodin: Finding the Living Spirit in Stone
item.description: An exhibition at the Louvre shows how the two artists, working centuries apart, found common ground in the vital force of rough-hewed textures.
item.dc:creator: Nina Siegal
item.media:credit: Photograph: Audrey Viger/Artworks: Musée du Louvre
item.media:description: The exhibition “Michelangelo and Rodin: Living Bodies” at the Louvre compares the techniques of the master artists who lived four centuries apart.

item.title: The Prince of Italian Pop Art Smiles Again
item.description: Lévy Gorvy Dayan gallery offers new glimpses of the perfectionism and hidden goofiness of Domenico Gnoli, whose caricatures of reality captured la dolce vita.
item.dc:creator: Walker Mimms

item.title: Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway and the ‘Devil Wears Prada’ Cast on the Sequel
item.description: Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, Stanley Tucci and the director David Frankel on “The Devil Wears Prada,” the sequel and who’s mean in real life.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik and Thea Traff
item.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times

item.title: Don Cheadle and Ayo Edebiri Are Giving ‘Proof’ a New Meaning
item.description: The actors connected quickly as they prepared to make their Broadway debuts in a new revival of David Auburn’s Pulitzer- and Tony-winning play.
item.dc:creator: Salamishah Tillet and Marcus Maddox
item.media:credit: Marcus Maddox for The New York Times

item.title: A Curator Died Suddenly. Could the Venice Biennale Be Saved?
item.description: Koyo Kouoh, who died of cancer at 57, was just months into her dream job overseeing the Venice Biennale’s centerpiece exhibition. But she left a plan that her assistants have tried to realize.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall
item.category: Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa
item.media:credit: Tsele Nthane for The New York Times
item.media:description: Koyo Kouoh in South Africa in 2023. Her husband said that curating the Venice Biennale’s centerpiece exhibition was Kouoh’s ultimate goal.

item.title: ‘Swapped’ Review: It’s Freaky Friday in the Animal Kingdom
item.description: Michael B. Jordan voices a woodland creature who swaps species with a bird in this middling animated movie on Netflix.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: Seth Meyers Gives Trump an Earful About Ear Jokes
item.description: The “Late Night” host expressed secondhand embarrassment after the president referred to the “beautiful ears” and “super hearing” of a NASA official.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: ‘Moby Dick’ Review: Robert Wilson’s Last Masterpiece
item.description: Wilson’s 2024 adaptation of Herman Melville’s classic, with music by the British singer-songwriter Anna Calvi, has a short run at the Brooklyn Academy of Music.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: Christopher Nell, left, as the sublime Boy and Rosa Enskat as Captain Ahab in Robert Wilson’s “Moby Dick” at the Brooklyn Academy of Music.

item.title: New Banksy Statue Causes Stir in Central London
item.description: The statue depicts a man marching with a flag that covers his face. It appeared in a section of London near statues of 19th-century British military and colonial figures.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.media:credit: Kin Cheung/Associated Press
item.media:description: The artist Banksy appeared to have taken credit for statue of a man holding a flag covering his face that stood in Waterloo Place in London.

item.title: Britney Spears Is Charged With D.U.I. Involving Drugs and Alcohol
item.description: Officers in California observed the pop star driving fast and erratically in March. After she was arrested, she checked herself into a treatment facility.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Julia Jacobs
item.media:credit: Eduardo Munoz/Reuters
item.media:description: Britney Spears was in a conservatorship for 13 years because of what her father said were public mental health struggles and possible substance abuse.

item.title: ‘Widow’s Bay’ Promises a Cursed Vacation
item.description: In this off-kilter horror-comedy on Apple TV, a seaside village with eccentric citizens and a troubled past becomes a tourist hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Jen Chaney
item.media:credit: Apple TV
item.media:description: Matthew Rhys is the mayor of a possibly cursed island community in “Widow’s Bay.”

item.title: Georg Baselitz, German Neo-Expressionist Painter, Dies at 88
item.description: Along with contemporaries like Anselm Kiefer, he mounted a frontal attack on Minimalism and Conceptualism, the dominant “cool” styles of the 1970s.
item.dc:creator: William Grimes

item.title: Dean Tavoularis, 93, Oscar-Winning Production Designer for Coppola, Dies
item.description: He worked on 13 Francis Ford Coppola films, including “The Godfather” trilogy and “Apocalypse Now,” and recreated the Las Vegas Strip for “One From the Heart.”
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.media:credit: Barbara Alper/Getty Images
item.media:description: Dean Tavoularis on the set of “The Brink’s Job” in 1978. He received his fifth Academy Award nomination for his work on the film, directed by William Friedkin.

item.title: David Allan Coe, Singer Who Personified Outlaw Country, Dies at 86
item.description: Mr. Coe, who wrote “Take This Job and Shove It” and other hits, was a transgressive exponent of the outlaw country movement of the 1970s and ’80s.
item.dc:creator: Bill Friskics-Warren
item.media:credit: Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images
item.media:description: David Allan Coe around 1970. From his debut, he registered as an original with an offbeat sense of humor, a deep baritone and a fierce resolve to be different.

item.title: Review: RZA’s ‘One Spoon of Chocolate’ Hammers Away at Racism
item.description: In RZA’s new movie, Unique (Shameik Moore) realizes Black residents in town are being hunted by white men. Despite the premise, the film lacks intensity.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: Is Yoko Ono Still Our Most Radical Artist?
item.description: In the 1960s, she invited an audience to cut off her clothes. As attacks on women’s rights escalate, “Cut Piece” and other decades-old works of feminist art feel more relevant than ever.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Fortini

item.title: ‘Two Pianos’ Review: Striking an Odd Chord
item.description: The filmmaker Arnaud Desplechin directed this off-kilter drama about a French pianist and the life he left behind.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: Review: ‘That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Tears of the Azure Sea’
item.description: Based on a popular anime series, the film, subtitled “Tears of the Azure Sea,” is a satisfactory stand-alone fantasy adventure set on an island resort.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Rapold
item.category: That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Tears of the Azure Sea (Movie)

item.title: ‘Hokum’ Review: Adam Scott Confronts His Demons and an Ancient Witch
item.description: Adam Scott is perfection as a damaged writer battling an ancient witch and his own demons in this hugely enjoyable chiller.
item.dc:creator: Jeannette Catsoulis

item.title: ‘Deep Water’ Review: Prepare for an Emergency Landing, With Fins
item.description: The action director Renny Harlin combines two different kinds of disaster movies, with lots of gory shark scenes.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: Ilana Glazer’s Next Act Goes Broader
item.description: She has a new podcast, stand-up hour and political organizing group, and is raising a 4-year-old daughter. But don’t worry, she still loves weed.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:credit: Aundre Larrow for The New York Times

item.title: Josh Johnson Gives King Charles Props for His Comedy Chops
item.description: “You know, I’ve always wondered what white Def Jam would be like,” Johnson said after King Charles cracked a few jokes at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: D4vd Bought Chain Saws to Dispose of Celeste Rivas Hernandez’s Body, Prosecutors Say
item.description: In a legal filing, prosecutors outlined for the first time how they say the singer murdered and dismembered Celeste Rivas Hernandez.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens, Emmanuel Morgan and Orlando Mayorquín
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A neighbor of Celeste Rivas Hernandez’s at a memorial in Lake Elsinore, Calf.

item.title: Roger Sweet, Creator of the He-Man Action Figure, Dies at 91
item.description: The musclebound character he developed as a toy designer for Mattel gave rise to the Masters of the Universe franchise and helped define the machismo of the 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

item.title: Queen Camilla Unites Winnie-the-Pooh With a Long-Lost Friend
item.description: On Wednesday, the queen of Britain presented the New York Public Library with a bespoke replica of Roo, the smallest companion of the Bear of Very Little Brain.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyall
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Chris Jackson
item.media:description: Queen Camilla presents a specially made replica of Roo, the baby kangaroo companion of Winnie-the-Pooh, to Anthony Marx, the president and chief executive of the New York Public Library.

item.title: Tupac Shakur’s Stepbrother Files Wrongful Death Lawsuit, 29 Years After Murder
item.description: Maurice Shakur said in a complaint filed against Duane Keith Davis, who was charged in 2023 with the rapper’s murder, that the “threads are starting to come together.”
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.media:credit: Associated Press
item.media:description: The rapper Tupac Shakur was shot four times in a drive-by shooting in Las Vegas in 1996. He died days later at the age of 25.

item.title: U.S. and Italy Honor Alliance to Curb Art Looting, Amid Broader Tensions
item.description: Standing near dozens of repatriated artifacts, officials from the countries celebrated efforts that have led to the return of thousands of artworks to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Some Joy to Your Day
item.description: Showering at the airport, blossoms in seams and the bar for joy.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: ‘Evita,’ Starring Rachel Zegler, Is Heading to Broadway
item.description: A buzzy revival of Andrew Lloyd Webber’s hit 1970s musical will transfer to New York next spring, but without its signature outdoor scene.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Marc Brenner
item.media:description: Rachel Zegler won an Olivier Award this month for her performance in “Evita” in London. Now she plans to return to the production when it heads to Broadway.

item.title: Three Great Documentaries to Stream
item.description: In this month’s picks, a notorious senator gets grilled, a man changes his eye color and Russian journalists struggle to do their jobs under authoritarianism.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: The New York Philharmonic Gives Gustavo Dudamel a Warm Welcome
item.description: The flamboyant conductor Gustavo Dudamel got a jubilant reception at the New York Philharmonic’s spring gala on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Dolly Faibyshev
item.category: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times

item.title: Visconti’s ‘Bellissima’: Mama’s Going to See to It!
item.description: The director’s 1951 movie, at Film Forum in a new 4K restoration, satirizes the Italian film industry via Anna Magnani’s over-the-top stage mother.
item.dc:creator: J. Hoberman
item.media:credit: Film Forum
item.media:description: Anna Magnani, right, plays a mother determined to make her child, Maria (Tina Apicella), a star.

item.title: ‘Departures’ Review: Finding Levity Amid the Pain
item.description: This tragicomedy from Lloyd Eyre-Morgan and Neil Ely balances a mordantly funny deconstruction of romance with the harsher realities of gay life.
item.dc:creator: Chris Azzopardi

item.title: 26 New Books to Read in May: Matt Haig, Carley Fortune, David Sedaris and More
item.description: Novels by Matt Haig, Elizabeth Strout and Carley Fortune; explosive true crime; immersive new fantasy; essays by David Sedaris; and more.
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Spills the Tea on Trump’s Royal Meeting
item.description: The late night show host called the king of England “one of the very few people who might actually be able to tip things even slightly in a positive direction.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: ‘Dances With Wolves’ Actor Is Sentenced to Life in Prison
item.description: Nathan Chasing Horse had been convicted of charges including the sexual assault of women and girls and the possession of child sexual abuse imagery.
item.dc:creator: Reggie Ugwu
item.media:credit: John Locher/Associated Press
item.media:description: Nathan Chasing Horse, in court in January. He had pleaded not guilty to all charges against him.

item.title: Takeaways From The Times’s Taylor Swift Interview on ‘Our Song,’ ‘All Too Well’ and More
item.description: Swift spoke with The New York Times about confessional songwriting, the inspiration behind some of her biggest hits and a writing quirk she can’t stand.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin

item.title: Kennedy Center Works to Make the Case That Repairs Are Urgent
item.description: In Washington and in federal court, the center is arguing that its planned two-year closure is crucial. Critics say it’s a result of declining attendance and fleeing artists.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and Zach Montague
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Among the changes the Trump administration has brought to the Kennedy Center is the addition of the president’s name to the exterior. Now it is slated to close for reasons that are the subject of debate.

item.title: What to Know About the Kimmel vs. Trump Feud
item.description: The president and first lady took exception to a joke. Jimmy Kimmel defended it.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jimmy Kimmel said of a line that annoyed the president: “It obviously was a joke.”

item.title: 11 Great American Songwriters Who Didn’t Make Our List
item.description: The New York Times Magazine named the 30 Greatest Living American Songwriters. Here are more contenders, including Billy Joel, Frank Ocean and Neko Case.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz
item.category: Hurray for the Riff Raff (Music Group)
item.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times
item.media:description: Billy Joel

item.title: ‘Running Point’ Is ‘The Office’ Meets the Lakers
item.description: The Netflix comedy is produced by the Lakers executive Jeanie Buss and based loosely on her life. Season 2 arrives as both she and the team are in flux.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Morgan

item.title: George Clooney Calls for Unity After White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting
item.description: The actor, who was honored at Film at Lincoln Center’s Chaplin Award Gala, denounced political violence after the attempted assassination of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Krista Schlueter for The New York Times

item.title: Laura Linney to Return to Broadway in New David Hare Play
item.description: The Manhattan Theater Club production will bring the actress back to the stage next spring, four years after her last Broadway production.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Gary Gershoff/Getty Images For Housing Works
item.media:description: Laura Linney will star as a writer in David Hare’s new play, “Montauk.”

item.title: In Replaced, a Sentient A.I. Learns Its Limits
item.description: For better or worse, the retro Replaced feels like a Sega Genesis game made with current technology.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Byrd

item.title: Geirr Tveitt’s Music Captured Norway’s Colors. His Life Reflected Its Shadows.
item.description: Geirr Tveitt’s reputation was tarnished by his nationalist politics. But a new generation of musicians is bringing this composer’s music to an international audience.
item.dc:creator: Corinna da Fonseca-Wollheim
item.media:credit: Aage Storløkken/Alamy
item.media:description: The Norwegian composer Geirr Tveitt in 1973, a few years after he lost about 80 percent of his compositions in a fire.

item.title: With ‘Sister Dreamer’ Park, Lauren Halsey Brings Her Heroes Home
item.description: The artist’s installation in South Central Los Angeles elevates familiar faces in the neighborhood with monuments of stone.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pogrebin and Amanda Hakan
item.media:credit: Amanda Hakan for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Flower Drum Song,’ Once Overhauled, Returns Changed Again
item.description: David Henry Hwang revised the Rodgers & Hammerstein classic 25 years ago. Now he gets to remedy some of his own remake’s flaws.
item.dc:creator: Robert Ito
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: David Henry Hwang’s revival of Rodgers and Hammerstein’s “Flower Drum Song” has returned to Los Angeles 25 years after it first premiered.

item.title: ‘The House of the Spirits’ Returns a Beloved Book to Its Origins
item.description: Shot in Chile, this series will be the first Spanish-language screen adaptation of the Isabel Allende novel. The star Alfonso Herrera said the story is more relevant than ever.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Aguilar
item.category: The House of the Spirits (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Martina Tuaty for The New York Times
item.media:description: Alfonso Herrera first read “The House of the Spirits” in high school. Now he is starring in a lavish adaptation on Amazon.

item.title: She’s Blazing a Trail for a Traditional Korean Zither in Jazz
item.description: The Seoul-born gayageum player DoYeon Kim has become an in-demand collaborator for some of the biggest innovators in improvised music.
item.dc:creator: Shaun Brady and Vincent Alban
item.media:description: “I need to figure out why I was born in this time, in this place, and why I chose music.” Kim said. “There must be a reason and a way I can use it to serve the world.”

item.title: The Obamas, Kim Kardashian and Other A-Listers Are Now Broadway Co-Producers
item.description: The latest trend on Broadway is celebrity co-producing: A-listers who now have credits as backers of plays and musicals.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Illustration by Max-o-matic. Photographs via Pexels/Loquellano (street sign); Unsplash/Barry Weatherall (curtain); Getty Images

item.title: Late Night Downloads the White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: The event “was supposed to be an evening of fun and merriment,” Jon Stewart said, “until, like most things in America, it was interrupted by gunfire.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Megan Thee Stallion to Leave ‘Moulin Rouge!’ on Broadway Early
item.description: The rapper will perform in “Moulin Rouge!” for the final time on Friday, though the production didn’t say why she was leaving more than two weeks early.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Herrington
item.media:credit: Theo Wargo/Getty Images For Hot Girl Productions
item.media:description: Megan Thee Stallion made her Broadway debut in “Moulin Rouge! The Musical” on March 24, and had been scheduled to appear through May 17.

item.title: Nedra Talley Ross, Last Surviving Member of the Ronettes, Dies at 80
item.description: With towering hairdos and perfect harmonies, she and her cousins Ronnie and Estelle brought a fresh edge to the girl-group sound in hits like “Be My Baby.”
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.media:credit: Art Zelin/Getty Images
item.media:description: Nedra Talley (later Nedra Talley Ross) around 1970. The Ronettes, the trio in which she was joined by two of her cousins, helped define the producer Phil Spector’s revolutionary “wall of sound.”
